completionNotes: Completed as a known-value autocomplete pattern with combobox-style popup suggestions, explicit selected-value state, invalid free-text handling, autocomplete-vs-search-suggestions distinction, source-backed examples, and screenshot spot-check evidence.

completionEvidence.patternSpecificGuidance: Guidance names known-value entry, editable combobox popup behavior, highlighted matching options, selected value state, no-match state, and validation when free text is not allowed.
completionEvidence.relatedPatternDistinction: The entry separates autocomplete from search suggestions for open-ended query formulation and from select when the option set is short enough to show directly.
completionEvidence.liveDemoBehavior: The live demo filters known customer values from typed text, lets users commit a specific option into the input, exposes selected status, supports clearing, and warns when continuing without a known selected value.
completionEvidence.comparisonDecisionRules: The linked search-suggestions-vs-autocomplete comparison distinguishes several possible query paths from constrained known values such as addresses, products, people, or codes.
completionEvidence.duplicateAudit: npm run audit:completion compares normalized guidance fields across patterns and currently reports no duplicate field group blocking this completed pattern.
completionEvidence.manualSpotCheck: screenshots/patterns/autocomplete.png was inspected after rendering and shows Good UI, Bad UI, Good UX, Bad UX, known-value popup behavior, selected value feedback, source-backed guidance, and comparison links.

name: Autocomplete
problem: Users need to enter a known value quickly without memorizing exact spelling, codes, or names.
solution: Let users type into an input and choose from matching known values, while keeping typed input visible and editable.
problemContext: The accepted values come from a known or searchable dataset.
Typing part of the value is faster than browsing the whole list.
selectionRules: Choose autocomplete for constrained or semi-constrained values.
Keep free text valid only when the domain permits it.
Show enough context to distinguish similar suggestions.
interactionContract: Typed text remains under user control.
Selecting an option updates the input and selected value consistently.
The popup state is exposed to keyboard and assistive technology users.
implementationChecklist: Use native datalist where sufficient or an accessible combobox implementation.
Expose active option and expanded state.
Handle slow remote results and stale responses.
Validate final value according to domain rules.
commonMisuses: Forcing the first suggestion when the user did not choose it.
Using autocomplete where users expect broad search results.
Showing identical labels without disambiguating context.
critiqueQuestions: Is the user completing a known value rather than discovering unknown content?
useWhen: The list is long but values are known.
Users can identify the target by typing part of it.
avoidWhen: The task is open-ended query exploration.
The option set is short enough to show as radio buttons.
accessibility: Expose combobox expanded state, active descendant, and option labels.
Keep typed text perceivable when suggestions appear.
keyboardBehavior: Escape closes the popup without clearing the input.
failureModes: Submitting stale selected IDs after label changes.

sources.note: Ontario autocomplete guidance supports text input with popup suggestions, combobox semantics, keyboard behavior, and known-value selection.
APG combobox guidance defines autocomplete popup behavior and keyboard expectations.

uiGuidance: Render a labeled text field, suggestion popup, highlighted option, selected value, and no-match state.
Expose clear visual states for expanded, focused, highlighted, and selected options.
uxGuidance: Help users complete a known value faster without forcing an incorrect suggestion.
Preserve typed input and make selection reversible before submission.

good: Persistent label above the input, readable text size, clear popup alignment, and visible highlighted option.
Suggestions include enough secondary text or spacing to distinguish similar values without crowding.
bad: Placeholder-only label that disappears after typing.
Popup visually detached from the field, tiny options, or highlight shown only by low-contrast color.

good: Typing filters suggestions while preserving the exact typed value.
Clicking or pressing Enter on a suggestion commits it and keeps the final value editable.
bad: Automatically forcing the first suggestion into the field.
Submitting a stale hidden ID after the user edits the visible text.

examples.notes: Defines editable combobox behavior, list autocomplete forms, popup roles, selected values, and keyboard interaction.
Documents autocomplete as a text-input suggestion widget with combobox semantics and keyboard behavior.
